# Transcrição: 04-How To Set Up Reporting Columns 

- **Fonte:** 04-How To Set Up Reporting Columns 
- **Duração:** 12:28
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey guys, in today's training we're going to go over how to set up reporting columns and then within those reporting columns how to spot bottlenecks by understanding where things should convert out along the funnel. For today we're going to use a standard e-commerce store as our example. Alright, so in order to get to your reporting columns you need to be in the ads manager initially and then you just need to go down to this drop down menu here. So here you can see that there's some pre-made reporting columns already. You can see that this one is the performance column so it's just showing you some performance stats. You can check out delivery. So this is kind of more based on impressions, CPM, frequency and then you can also look at video engagement if you're running a lot of video campaigns. You can really check out a ton of different preset reporting columns. So here we're going to create some custom columns. So let's go to the custom columns tab here and then we can really just clear all of these because this is from a pre-made one. So if we clear all those we're going to leave delivery in here and errors and then we'll leave the campaign name in there as well because we can't change it. So the first things that we're going to want to look at here are going to be the CPM. So we're going to add in the CPM here and then we're also going to want to look at the impressions. So there's a couple of different ways that you can actually add these things in. You can see here you can scroll down and just click the button here. You can also search for them here. So sometimes it's easier to just search for them. So impressions and you can see if you uncheck that it'll go away and then you can also check it. So it'll show up here depending on which ones you select. It'll show up in the order that you select them. So the CPM is an important metric here and basically what it's telling you is how much it costs to get a thousand impressions on your ad or within your ad set. So it's really important to keep an eye on the CPM but it's not the be all and end all. Ultimately what you want to look at if you're looking at a conversion campaign for a e-commerce store you're going to want to look at that cost per purchase. If you're running a lead generation campaign you're going to want to look at that cost per lead. So ultimately you want to look at that end event and base your decisions on that. Instead of looking at the CPM and saying hey my CPM is too high I need to change that even though your lead cost might be okay or even though your cost per purchase might be okay for e-commerce. So those are the first two things you want to add in there is CPM and impressions. The next one you want to add in is the click through rate. So you want to make sure that you're adding the link click through rate and not just the basic click through rate here. So you want to add in the click through rate and you can search for that here. So you go link click through rate. So we're going to want to use the link click through rate because we're going to want to get an idea of the percentage of times people saw the ad and actually performed a link click. For this it's basically going to tell us how many times someone saw an ad or the percentage of times someone saw an ad and performed a click anywhere so they might have clicked

## [03:02 - 06:04]

on the image or anything like that. Whereas here it's specifically clicking on a link. So this is important because we ultimately want people to click on the link and that is more important than someone necessarily clicking on the image. So we're going to add that in to our reporting columns. Next we're going to want to add in the link clicks and we want to make sure that we're adding in link clicks but unique link clicks as well. So this is the number of people who performed a link click. This is the number of link clicks that happened on the ad and it could be the same person. So here this is unique link clicks so it's just they're tracking one user. So if one user went through and clicked on the same link three times it's only going to track them once here. So this is what we want to look at. And then the next step for e-commerce we're going to want to look at content views. So content views are basically going to be anyone who has looked at a certain product. And here what we want to add is the total and then we also want to look at the cost here. So these are just going to start to clutter your columns your reporting columns as well so we can remove these right out of the reporting columns and then we can move these down here. Well actually we'll keep that guy up here. So we're looking at link clicks, we're looking at content views, we're looking at cost per content view and then what we're going to add in is the add to carts. So what we're doing here is we're breaking down the reporting columns based on where people are at in the funnel. So initially what we're going to want to do is we're going to want to see how much it's costing for people to actually see the ad. And then that's what's being tracked by the CPM and the impressions. Now beyond that we want to see out of those people who have seen the ad how many people are actually clicking on the link. So this click through rate is what we're going to be using to track that and then this is telling us exactly how many unique link clicks we got from that. Now beyond that once people have clicked on the ad they're going to be either going to a product page or the home page. So here we set up content views, you can also add in landing page views as well. Landing page views is slightly less accurate but you can add in landing page views as well just for the purposes of this training. And then people have viewed that content, okay they viewed a certain product, in this case maybe they viewed a t-shirt. The next step is they're going to add to cart, right? So we want to know how much that view content cost and then we want to know how many people out of that, out of the people who viewed content, how many people added to cart. So we're adding the add to cart in there and then we also want to know how much that add to cart costs us to get as well. Now depending on what platform you're using you might want to add in more metrics as well in here, you might want to add in initiate checkout or checkouts initiated. For Shopify these are standardized so you can't change them but it's still interesting

## [06:04 - 09:07]

to look at. So here you have checkouts initiated and then you have the cost per checkout initiated. Now again with Shopify this is standardized so you can't change anything. So typically I leave this out of my reporting columns but if we're using a platform other than Shopify where you might be able to change and tweak the checkout page, this is something that can be really useful. The next metric in our reporting columns that we should add is purchase. So we're adding purchases in there and then we want to know what the cost per purchase is. We can just remove these and this is you can add offline purchases if you're tracking offline purchases. For the most part a lot of us aren't going to be doing a lot of offline purchase tracking so I usually just remove this from the columns just because it declutters some of what we're looking at. And then we have the cost per purchase here and then after the cost per purchase we're going to want to look at the ROAS which is already pulled up here. So we have the total ROAS and again we can remove that just to declutter things. And then the next step we want to look at how much money we spent. So the amount spent here. And then finally we want to look at the frequency. So those are the main reporting columns that you're going to want to have specifically with e-commerce. Now you can save this and actually another thing that you're going to want to include So these are the main reporting columns you're going to want to use if you're running an e-commerce funnel. Now there are a few other columns that you're going to want to include. We're going to create a separate training for these but in the past you would also look at the relevant score as well. There's new metrics that are available now and we're going to look at those new metrics in a different training but for the purposes of this training these are the main metrics that we're going to look at. So we're going to save this as a preset so basically saving it as a preset is going to make it so that you can pull this up anytime so you don't have to go through this process every time you want to look at these reporting metrics. So we're going to save this as e-commerce funnel and then apply. So now you can see here we've got all the reporting columns set up according to what we want to see. So we're going to collapse this section here the campaign name because this is not super important right now. And then we can look at okay we can look at the CPM we can look at the impressions the click-through rate the unique link clicks the landing page views we can look at all of these different metrics and it's easily accessible. So you can see each step of the funnel as it flows from step to step. Another thing that's really helpful when you're looking at these reporting columns is knowing some industry average stats. So for e-commerce here are a couple industry average stats that you're going to want to look at. So the first one is the click-through rate. So for cold audiences you want to look at about a 1% click-through rate. So if this click-through rate right here is 1% or higher that's great. If it's lower than 1% then you're going to want to make some tweaks to your copy or your creative.

## [09:07 - 12:09]

Now for retargeting you want to make sure that your click-through rate is above 3. So usually you want to shoot for about a 3% or above 3% click-through rate because the thought process here is those people who are seeing these ads who are being retargeted with these ads are people who are of higher intent. So they should resonate more with what you're posting and a higher percentage of people who see it should be clicking on it. Now I just realized we missed a fundamental step in here so we want to include the cost per unique link click as well. So we have the unique link clicks but we also want to include the cost per unique link click. So we just go down here and we go customize column and then we want to add the cost per unique link click. So we have that here and then we want to add that right by, so you can actually drag and drop these as well. This is another great feature. They never used to have this but you can drag and drop depending on where you want this to go as well. So you can order it exactly how your funnel is set up. So here we want to drag this up to where unique link clicks are so you can see the cost per unique link click and then we want to save that as a preset again and we will apply that to that preset. It's going to ask us do you want to replace this? Yes, because I just wanted to add that column in and now we go back here and we look at the cost per unique link click and we're just going to give it a second here to load. Okay, so this is finally loaded here and now what we want to look at is the cost per unique link click. Here you want to shoot for a cost per unique link click under a dollar. So if you can get a dollar under that's great. If not, if you're above a dollar you're going to want to look at definitely changing up and testing some new copy and some new creative. So you might see a higher cost per link click on middle of funnel or bottom of funnel. Just keep an eye on that and make sure that your ROAS is okay on that as well. Now the next stat you're going to want to look at is the bounce rate. So an acceptable bounce rate is about 20% in e-commerce. So here we're looking at the unique link clicks versus the landing page views. And it's important to note that the landing page views are actually just an estimate based on what Facebook is estimating. But essentially what you want to look at is how many people who clicked on the link of that ad specifically went and landed on the page and actually waited until it loaded. So here we're looking at about a 9% bounce rate. So that looks pretty good for us considering the industry average is about 20%. One big metric we're going to want to look at on e-commerce sites is the conversion rate as well. So that means how many people go to your site actually purchase. So you should be shooting for between 2% and 3% on an e-commerce site. So that means for every 100 people who go to your site at least two or three are buying. So if we're using this as an example here and we're just scrolling to the right here we have 41 purchases out of about a thousand landing page visits.

## [12:09 - 12:28]

So we're looking at about a 4% conversion rate which is higher than average. So that's great for an e-commerce site. So that's how you set up your reporting columns and that's also how you can spot some bottlenecks by understanding where things should convert along your funnel. Be sure to watch the next video on how to interpret the new relevance quality scores.
